my_thoughts: 'I'd say about twenty percent'—honest quantification. Seven turns from defensive question ('Am I supposed to...?') to precise self-assessment ('twenty percent'). The teaching sequence: (1) track behavior relentlessly (T1-3), (2) shift to meta-level when resistance peaks (T4), (3) use professional standards analogy (T5), (4) force vague admission to precision (T6), (5) link percentage to outcome (T7). Clean teaching arc. The '20% here = 20% result' makes it THEIR problem: if you want better results, bring more involvement. Not 'you should' but 'input determines output.' Ends with invitation: 'What can we do about this 80% missing?' Collaborative, not judgmental. The episode worked: from unconscious avoidance to conscious quantified admission to problem-solving frame. Very efficient seven-turn sequence.
accumulated_insight: T7 (Final): Student quantifies: 'I'd say about twenty percent'—sober realization replaces vague admission. Seven-turn arc complete: guarded irritation (T1) -> justification (T2) -> passive withdrawal (T3) -> resistant boredom (T4) -> proforma defense (T5) -> caught in logic (T6) -> sober realization (T7). Teacher links input to outcome: '20% here = 20% result. What can we do about the 80% missing?' Uses causal chain to show consequence and invites collaboration. Episode ends with student quantifying their own resistance and teacher framing it as solvable problem (not character flaw).
visible_response:
What can we do about this eighty percent that’s missing here?
After all, if you’re only twenty percent here, you’ll just have a twenty percent result.
